operation: First, initialize a 2D sketch on the XY workplane, then offset the sketch origin to (-10, 0). Draw a 3-unit long vertical line upwards, followed by a three-point arc with middle control point at (10, 6) and end point at (20, 3), then draw a 3-unit long vertical line downwards to finish the upper half of the profile. Mirror this upper profile across the X-axis to form a closed symmetric 2D cross-section. Finally, extrude the closed cross-section symmetrically to a total depth of 30 units to generate the final 3D solid.
geometry: This is a uniform prismatic solid with constant cross-section along its extrusion direction, symmetric about the central plane running along its length. Its cross-section has straight vertical edges at the two narrow ends, and smooth convex curved arcs on the upper and lower sides, forming a rounded symmetric closed shape. The longitudinal faces along the extrusion length include flat faces matching the straight cross-section edges and curved ruled faces matching the cross-section arcs.
function: This symmetric curved profile extrusion is designed to be used as a spacer, wear pad, structural insert, or custom profile segment, featuring smooth curved contact surfaces and balanced symmetric geometry that supports even load distribution or fitting into curved mounting slots.
